articles.expand: The price of silver is under intense pressure today, tumbling $6.55 or -7.84% to $76.90. The sharp decline marks the largest one-day percentage drop since March 3, when the metal fell -8.15%, and reflects a dramatic shift in momentum after silver once again failed near a major resistance target around the $90 level. articles.title: Euro: Growth downgrades and softer outlook – Societe Generale
articles.expand: Societe Generale economists highlights that Eurozone 2026 GDP forecasts have been cut more than United States (US) projections in percentage terms, reinforcing United States (US) outperformance.

articles.title: Euro falls as hawkish Fed expectations boost the US Dollar, Treasury yields
articles.expand: The Euro (EUR) extends losses against the US Dollar (USD) on Friday, with EUR/USD slipping to near one-month lows as hawkish Federal Reserve (Fed) expectations boost the Greenback and US Treasury yields.

articles.title: Gold Weekly Forecast: Sellers return on hawkish Fed bets and Middle East uncertainty
articles.expand: Gold (XAU/USD) edged higher at the beginning of the week but made a sharp U-turn as the US Dollar (USD) gathered strength on hot inflation data, closing the week in the red by losing more than 3% of its value.

articles.title: Australian Dollar extends sharp decline as Fed hike repricing lifts US Dollar
articles.expand: AUD/USD falls sharply on Friday and trades around 0.7155 at the time of writing, down 0.91% on the day, after hitting its lowest level in more than a week. The pair remains under heavy selling pressure for the second consecutive day amid broad-based US Dollar (USD) strength.

articles.title: Japan: Solid GDP but JPY still weighed by trade – DBS
articles.expand: DBS economists Taimur Baig and Radhika Rao expect Japan’s 1Q Gross Domestic Product (GDP) to grow 1.8% QoQ saar, supported by firm exports and AI- and semiconductor-related investment, keeping their 0.5% full-year GDP forecast on track.

articles.title: Canada: Energy lifts CPI, BoC focus on core – TD Securities
articles.expand: TD Securities’ Senior Canada Economist Robert Both expects Canadian Consumer Price Index (CPI) inflation to rise to 3.1% year-on-year in April, driven mainly by higher energy and food prices and base effects from last year’s carbon tax changes.

articles.expand: Gold (XAU/USD) slides to over one-week lows on Friday, pressured by a stronger US Dollar (USD) and rising Treasury yields as mounting Oil-driven inflation risks strengthen expectations that the Federal Reserve (Fed) could keep interest rates elevated for longer.
